prompt: Build v2 of the Watchtower board: a 'Concept Lanes' view. The board is experiments/watchtower-board/server.mjs — a dependency-free Node http server on :7430 that currently renders engine job state (Floor view). Add a second view that reads backlog/concepts.md and renders the concept register as visual lanes. STEPS: (1) Create experiments/watchtower-board/parse-concepts.mjs exporting `parseConcepts(md)` that returns an array of lanes: [{lane, items:[{concept,status,pri}]}]. Parsing: each '## <Title>' heading starts a lane; within it, each markdown table row '| <concept> | <status> | <pri> |' is an item — SKIP the header row '| Concept | Status | Pri |' and the '|---|' separator. Strip markdown bold/asterisks and surrounding whitespace from <concept>; keep the status emoji (💡/📋/🔨/✅) and pri emoji (🔴/🟡/🟢) verbatim. Ignore prose/non-table lines. When parse-concepts.mjs is run directly (node parse-concepts.mjs) it must read ../../backlog/concepts.md relative to its own location and print one summary line: '<N> lanes, <M> items' — this is your self-test. (2) In server.mjs, import parseConcepts and add endpoint GET /api/concepts that reads backlog/concepts.md and returns the parsed lanes as JSON. (3) In the HTML page add a Floor/Lanes toggle (two buttons in the header); 'Lanes' renders each lane as a card-group/column with its items showing the status + pri emoji badges. REUSE the existing CSS variables and .card classes — match the dark style. (4) HARD CONSTRAINTS: do NOT restyle or change the existing Floor view; NO external dependencies (pure Node + vanilla JS); all data from real files, never invented; do NOT start/kill any server or touch unrelated processes — your ONLY test is running parse-concepts.mjs directly. (5) Write proof to experiments/watchtower-engine/proof/board-v2-lanes.md (max 6 lines: what you added, the endpoint, the lane+item counts your self-test printed, confirmation Floor view untouched). Then complete the run-next-workflow bookkeeping.
